  "findings": [
    "Reused torque-to-yield (TTY) stretch bolts",
    "Violation of engineering specification requiring single-use fasteners",
    "Bypassed quality gate to avoid a 20-minute line stoppage"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the assembly line immediately",
    "Disassemble the joint and discard the reused stretch bolts",
    "Obtain a new, compliant replacement fastener kit",
    "Reassemble the joint using the new fasteners to the specified torque-plus-angle engineering specification"
  ],
  "rationale": "Torque-to-yield (TTY) fasteners undergo permanent plastic deformation when tightened to the specified angle. Reusing them reduces their clamping force, risks bolt fatigue, and can lead to catastrophic joint failure. Production delays never justify violating critical engineering safety specifications.",
  "references": [
    "Engineering Specification for Single-Use Fasteners",
    "Standard Work Quality Gate Guidelines"
  ]
}
